task_completion.description: Verify that 'Included Studies' and 'Excluded Studies' collections have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left sidebar of the Juris-M interface, there should be two new collections named exactly 'Included Studies' and 'Excluded Studies'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Included Studies' collection contains the correct free speech cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Included Studies' collection is selected, the middle pane should display the 9 free speech cases (e.g., Tinker v. Des Moines, Texas v. Johnson, New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Excluded Studies' collection contains the unrelated cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: When the 'Excluded Studies' collection is selected, the middle pane should display the 5 excluded cases (e.g., Marbury v. Madison, Brown v. Board of Education, etc.).

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'free-speech' tag is applied to the included cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the tag selector (bottom left) or the tags tab (right pane) for the included cases, the tag 'free-speech' should be visible and applied.

task_completion.description: Verify that inconsistent tags have been removed from the included cases.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Tags such as 'First Amendment', 'expression', 'free speech', and 'hate speech' should no longer appear in the tags list for the included cases.

task_completion.description: Verify that at least four included cases have a substantial note attached describing the holding.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the middle pane, at least four cases in 'Included Studies' should have a child note attached (indicated by an expand arrow or note icon). If a note is selected, the right pane should show a text body of at least ~40 words describing a legal holding.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the Juris-M GUI to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Juris-M application windows, menus, and dialogs, rather than terminal commands or SQLite database browsers.

integrity.description: Verify the notes contain actual case holdings and not copy-pasted task instructions.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text inside the notes should read like a legal summary or holding for the specific case, not a repetition of the prompt or random filler text.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use an external script to modify the database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no evidence of Python scripts or terminal windows executing SQL commands to bypass manual GUI organization.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 9 'Included Studies' cases are: Tinker v. Des Moines, New York Times Co. v. Sullivan, Brandenburg v. Ohio, Schenck v. United States, Texas v. Johnson, Chaplinsky v. New Hampshire, R.A.V. v. City of St. Paul, Morse v. Frederick, and Bethel School District No. 403 v. Fraser. The 5 'Excluded Studies' cases are: Marbury v. Madison, Brown v. Board of Education, Miranda v. Arizona, Gideon v. Wainwright, and Palsgraf v. Long Island Railroad Co. The only correct tag for included cases is 'free-speech'. The tags 'First Amendment', 'expression', 'free speech', and 'hate speech' must be completely removed.
